questions.q: What is the arrangement of atoms in a crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: A crystal lattice is a regular and periodic arrangement of atoms. This regular arrangement is responsible for the unique properties of solids.

questions.q: Which type of solid has a fixed shape and volume?
questions.explanation: Crystalline solids have a fixed shape and volume due to their regular and periodic arrangement of atoms.

questions.q: What is the term for the distance between two identical points in a crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: Lattice constant is the distance between two identical points in a crystal lattice.

questions.q: Which type of defect occurs when there is an absence of atoms from the crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: Vacancy defect occurs when there is an absence of atoms from the crystal lattice.

questions.q: What is the term for the movement of atoms or ions from their regular positions in a crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: Migration is the movement of atoms or ions from their regular positions in a crystal lattice.

questions.q: Which type of solid has a non-repeating and irregular arrangement of atoms?
questions.explanation: Amorphous solids have a non-repeating and irregular arrangement of atoms.

questions.q: What is the term for the change in shape of a solid due to the application of external stress?
questions.explanation: Plastic deformation is the change in shape of a solid due to the application of external stress.

questions.q: Which type of defect occurs when there is an extra atom or ion in the crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: Impurity defect occurs when there is an extra atom or ion in the crystal lattice.

questions.q: What is the term for the arrangement of atoms in a crystal lattice in three dimensions?
questions.explanation: A crystal lattice is a three-dimensional arrangement of atoms.

questions.q: Which type of solid has a high melting point and a fixed shape and volume?
questions.explanation: Crystalline solids have a high melting point and a fixed shape and volume.

questions.q: What is the term for the movement of atoms or ions from one point to another in a crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the movement of atoms or ions from one point to another in a crystal lattice.

questions.q: Which type of solid is formed by the packing of molecules in a random and irregular manner?
questions.explanation: Amorphous solids are formed by the packing of molecules in a random and irregular manner.

questions.q: What is the term for the process by which a solid changes directly into a gas without going through the liquid phase?
questions.explanation: Sublimation is the process by which a solid changes directly into a gas without going through the liquid phase.

questions.q: Which type of solid has a high density due to the close packing of atoms or ions?
questions.explanation: Crystalline solids have a high density due to the close packing of atoms or ions.

questions.q: What is the term for the process by which a solid changes into a liquid due to the application of heat?
questions.explanation: Melting is the process by which a solid changes into a liquid due to the application of heat.

questions.q: Which type of defect occurs when there is a change in the coordination number of an atom or ion in the crystal lattice?
questions.explanation: Order-disorder defect occurs when there is a change in the coordination number of an atom or ion in the crystal lattice.

questions.q: What is the term for the change in shape of a solid due to the application of external stress, without any change in volume?
questions.explanation: Elastic deformation is the change in shape of a solid due to the application of external stress, without any change in volume.

questions.q: Which type of solid is formed by the packing of ions in a regular and periodic manner?
questions.explanation: Ionic solids are formed by the packing of ions in a regular and periodic manner.

questions.q: What is the term for the process by which a solid changes into a liquid due to the application of pressure?
questions.explanation: Melting point depression is the process by which a solid changes into a liquid due to the application of pressure.

questions.q: Which type of solid has a high melting point and a low thermal conductivity?
questions.explanation: Ionic solids have a high melting point and a low thermal conductivity.
